item.title: Plastics Shortages to Keep Food Prices High After War Disruptions
item.description: Asian consumers will be facing higher grocery bills for months thanks to costly packaging stemming from the Iran war, which created severe shortages of plastics needed to get food from farms to market.

item.title: US Stock Futures Surge on Micron’s Strong Forecast: Markets Wrap
item.description: US share futures gained after Micron Technology Inc.’s upbeat outlook reignited confidence in the artificial-intelligence trade, helping fuel a late rally on Wall Street.

item.title: Oil Holds Drop After Erasing Most Wartime Gains as Supply Swells
item.description: Oil was steady following a run of losses that’s erased most of the wartime price gains, with fears of a supply crunch subsiding as more ships transit the Strait of Hormuz after early progress on a peace deal.

item.title: Lula Ally Leaves Senate Leadership Job Amid Ties to Bank Scandal
item.description: The Brazilian Senate leader from President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s party stepped down from his post after a police investigation uncovered connections to failed lender Banco Master.

item.title: Crypto Deal Between Cantor SPAC and Adam Back DAT Gets Delayed
item.description: A closely watched vote on a tie-up between a Cantor Fitzgerald-linked company and a digital-asset treasury firm led by Adam Back has been delayed.

item.title: Bain Capital Nears Deal for Stake in Volkswagen’s Everllence Unit
item.description: Bain Capital is nearing a deal to buy a controlling stake in Volkswagen AG’s heavy diesel engine unit and end one of Europe’s most hotly-contested private equity auctions, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Micron and Qualcomm Lift Chipmakers Amid Rapid AI Growth
item.description: Shares of semiconductor stocks are soaring in extended trading on Wednesday, following a strong financial report from Micron Technology Inc. that underscored how artificial intelligence remains a major vector for growth.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: AI人材流出続く、米国上場を申請、首位返り咲き視野、「青天井」シナリオ、供給過剰の兆し

item.title: Qualcomm Projects Data Center Chip Sales of $15 Billion by 2029
item.description: Qualcomm Inc., trying to expand its dominance from mobile phone chips, forecast sales of more than $15 billion a year by fiscal 2029 in the booming market for AI components in data centers.

item.title: Micron Forecast Shatters Estimates on Insatiable AI Demand
item.description: Micron Technology Inc., the largest US maker of computer memory chips, delivered a sales forecast that topped Wall Street estimates after AI-fueled shortages of the components sent prices soaring.

item.title: Big Banks Pass Fed Stress Test, Paving Way for Payouts
item.description: All of the biggest US banks cleared the Federal Reserve’s annual stress test, setting the stage for lenders to boost buybacks and dividends.

item.title: Nvidia, SpaceX Drive US High-Grade Bond Sales to a June Record
item.description: US investment-grade bond sales set another record in June, fueled by voracious investor demand and a wave of borrowing tied to the artificial intelligence spending boom.

item.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of ‘Illicitly’ Accessing AI Models
item.description: Anthropic PBC accused Chinese technology giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. of waging a large-scale effort to “illicitly” access its Claude artificial intelligence model using thousands of fraudulent accounts that undermine the US AI developer’s decision to keep its products out of China.

item.title: Qualcomm Announces New AI Chips, Adds Meta as Customer
item.description: Qualcomm Inc. said Meta Platforms Inc. has agreed to use the mobile phone chipmaker’s new data center processor in its infrastructure, suggesting the company is gaining a foothold in the booming market for AI gear.

item.title: Alphabet’s Dow Debut Shows Index Headache in Tech-Driven Economy
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s addition to the Dow Jones Industrial Average marks another step in the benchmark’s effort to catch up with a market increasingly defined by Big Tech.

item.title: Fed Says Basis Trade Key Driver of Hedge Fund Treasury Exposure
item.description: Hedge funds’ growing exposure to Treasuries is primarily due to the revival of the cash-futures basis trade, according to the Federal Reserve.

item.title: SPAC Deals Seen as Key Path to Public Market in Data Center Boom
item.description: The rebound in blank-check companies is proving to be a useful route to public markets for companies involved in the artificial intelligence-data center build-out, said Betsy Cohen, a veteran of SPAC dealmaking.

item.title: Google Poised to Lose Two More High-Profile AI Staffers to Anthropic
item.description: Two leading artificial intelligence researchers at Alphabet Inc.’s Google are planning to leave for rival Anthropic PBC, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a series of high-profile departures that risk undercutting the search giant’s position in AI.Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel, both viewed internally as key contributors to Google’s Gemini AI model, are set to move to the Claude maker, said the people, who spoke on condition of anonymity as the information is not public.

item.title: Ex-Tricolor COO to Plead Guilty to Fraud in Firm’s Collapse
item.description: Former Tricolor Holdings Chief Operating Officer David Goodgame told a judge that he will plead guilty to fraud charges stemming from the used-car dealer and lender’s collapse last year.

item.title: Bitcoin Breaks $60,000 Again as Crypto’s Biggest Buyers Wobble
item.description: Bitcoin’s slide back below $60,000 is reviving a fear that has largely been absent from crypto for the past two years: what happens when the market’s biggest buyer comes under pressure just as retail traders lose interest.

item.title: Warren to Call for Antitrust Scrutiny of AI, Deal Review Revamp
item.description: Massachusetts Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren is planning to urge Congress to push for greater antitrust enforcement on emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and revamp merger law to make more information public.

item.title: Sony Pictures Invests $100 Million in Giant Screen Startup Cosm
item.description: Sony Pictures Entertainment is investing $100 million in Cosm, an operator of large-screen theaters that show films and sporting events.

item.title: ECB’s Zigman Says Price Stability Is Key, Cheaper Oil Will Help
item.description: The top priority for the European Central Bank is keeping inflation in check, a task that’s becoming a little easier as oil prices retreat, according to Governing Council member Ante Zigman.

item.title: SpaceX Holder Andreessen Says Starship Opens Path to AI in Space
item.description: Andreessen Horowitz LLC General Partner David George, an investor in SpaceX, said the “rapid reusability” of the new Starship rocket will be crucial to delivering on Elon Musk’s bet on orbital data centers to expand AI computing capacity.

item.title: Fed’s Bowman Finalizes Moves to Reshape Bank-Supervision Unit
item.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman has completed a shakeup of the agency’s bank-oversight unit as she aims to prioritize what she sees as core financial risks.

item.title: Hormuz Reopening Is Quickly Flooding Oil Markets With Supply
item.description: Key parts of the oil market are suddenly awash in supply, as a stream of cargoes out of the Strait of Hormuz accelerates after the US-Iran agreement to open the waterway.

item.title: Bank of Canada Rejects Recession Label for Economy’s Weakness
item.description: Bank of Canada officials rejected the notion that the country’s economy is in recession as they set borrowing costs earlier this month, though they acknowledged weak growth and labor market slack.

item.title: Remembering the Debasement Trade
item.description: Let’s get a temperature check.

item.title: Stonepeak Raises $2.5 Billion in Private Bonds Tied to LNG Plant
item.description: Stonepeak has sold roughly $2.5 billion of private bonds to refinance a loan related to its investment in a liquefied natural gas export terminal in Louisiana.

item.title: Subzero Natural Gas Prices Show Need for Energy Permit Reform, Shale CEO Says
item.description: A record string of negative natural gas prices in the biggest US shale field shows that the US needs to streamline permitting for pipelines and other energy infrastructure, according to the boss of oil and gas producer Ovintiv Inc.
